old_claim.note: Clay Gathering and Preparation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Mud brick uses clay, sand, silt, water, and sometimes fibrous temper, so clay-rich material preparation is enabling material practice.

source_shape.source_locator: Britannica defines mud brick as clay, sand, and silt combined with water and sometimes fibrous material, shaped into bricks and dried in the sun.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Mud brick is made from prepared clay-rich sediment with water and optional fibrous temper.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source identifies the material preparation inputs, supporting clay preparation as an enabling material practice rather than a generic inferred prerequisite.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: clay_gathering_and_preparation remains an enabling material prerequisite for sun-dried mud brick.
would_reject_if: The node is later narrowed to brick use only, with no material-production claim.
A stronger source shows the earliest scoped bricks were not clay-rich mud or sediment units.
The clay_gathering_and_preparation node is narrowed to pottery-only processing and no longer covers clay-rich building materials.
short_reason: Mud brick production directly uses prepared clay-rich sediment and temper.
